settings.label: Interrupt GPIO (none = polling)
settings.comment: Pin set -> interrupt mode: the driver writes G_MODE=trigger (0xA4=1) and the firmware reads the chip only when the INT line signals a touch (no I2C while idle). No pin -> polling mode (G_MODE=0, periodic read). The pin is claimed in the shared GPIO owner map (conflict-checked, shown in the pinmap).

settings.label: Reset GPIO
settings.comment: Pin set -> the firmware pulses it at startup per the driver's reset_timing (active-low). No pin -> not pulsed (e.g. the reset line is shared with the panel and cycled by the display init). Claimed in the shared GPIO owner map.

settings.label: Touch threshold
settings.comment: Written to TH_GROUP (0x80) at init. Touch-detection threshold; lower = more sensitive. 22 is a typical FT6336U value — tune per panel.

settings.label: Active report period
settings.comment: Written to PERIODACTIVE (0x88) at init. Scan/report period in active mode (lower = faster reporting, more power). 12 is a typical FT6336U value.

settings.label: Swap X/Y axes
settings.comment: Orientation (applied by the display handler, not the chip). Transpose: use the touch X as the display Y and vice versa — for a panel scanned 90 deg rotated vs the display. Applied before mirror.

settings.label: Mirror X
settings.comment: Flip the X axis against the display width (after any swap).

settings.label: Mirror Y
settings.comment: Flip the Y axis against the display height (after any swap).
